generatedSentence: Dr. Vesper, an early and elect genius in science, often went hiking. Her imperfect, ovoid sculpture of a swan on her desk, beside a vibrant scarf, was a reminder of her debut into ornithology. Her ocarina was often neglected, as her mind was too busy to be ungrounded from her work. She had a halcyon dream of flight. Suddenly, the ground started shaking, and a strange emitter began to pulse with a hazy, inchoate light. A monstrous, berserk beast, its fur partially burned, emerged with a terrifying roaring. Dr. Vesper felt a piercing tinnitus and a pang of regret for her previous peaceful studies near the rivulet. The creature, now banding together with others, seemed ungrateful for their existence. She knew she had to inject something to stop them. An eidolon of fear gripped her. With a brilliant flash, Dr. Vesper – now known as "The Vesper" – made a swift judgement. She wore a heavy vest and her heraldic symbol gleamed as she launched an ultraviolet beam from a device she called 'The Jarl'. The beam's density was immense, designed to disrupt the creatures. The air shimmered, reminiscent of a distant volcano. She had documented a similar ritual on ancient vellum, detailing how a 'King's Jeroboam' device could be jokingly called upon. She held her enemy with steely regard. A nearby derrick glowed from the energy. The monstrous form was effectively deleted from reality. Dr. Vesper, now a jumper of dimensions, saw a tiny iceblink on a distant peak, a memory of her original world where she'd once seen an icefish. She felt a new sense of calm, like a dragonfly hovering over a quiet vineyard. The world had to uprise from the chaos. Later, she would telephone her colleagues, perhaps enjoying a bowl of oatmeal with onion and discussing how a tactful approach, like a good tailor fits a suit, had saved them. A quiet twinkle in the sky heralded a new era, secured by a single euro she had found. A small uakari monkey watched from a tree, a symbol of newfound peace.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, dynamic superhero comic book style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Dr. Vesper, wearing a crimson, flowing scarf and a silver gauntlet on her right arm. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Dr. Vesper, a woman with a focused expression, looking at an ovoid swan sculpture on a desk, a subtle glow on her face, extreme close-up. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A monstrous, shadowy beast with glowing red eyes emerging from a hazy, pulsing portal, ground shaking violently, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Dr. Vesper, poised and determined, firing an ultraviolet beam from a gauntlet-like device towards the roaring beast, dramatic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Dr. Vesper, standing amidst calm, with a dragonfly hovering nearby, looking towards a distant iceblink on a peaceful landscape, eye-level shot. cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting.

definitions.definition: Any of various species of large, long-necked waterfowl, of genus Cygnus (bird family: Anatidae), most of which have white plumage.
One whose grace etc. suggests a swan.
This bird used as a heraldic charge, sometimes with a crown around its neck (e. g. the arms of Buckinghamshire).
To travel or move about in an aimless, idle, or pretentiously casual way.
A long, often knitted, garment worn around the neck.
A headscarf.
A neckcloth or cravat.
To throw on loosely; to put on like a scarf.
To dress with a scarf, or as with a scarf; to cover with a loose wrapping.
A particular discipline or branch of learning, especially one dealing with measurable or systematic principles rather than intuition or natural ability.
definitions.example: Of course in my opinion Social Studies is more of a science than an art.

definitions.definition: Specifically the natural sciences.
definitions.example: My favorite subjects at school are science, mathematics, and history.

definitions.definition: Knowledge gained through study or practice; mastery of a particular discipline or area.
The fact of knowing something; knowledge or understanding of a truth.
The collective discipline of study or learning acquired through the scientific method; the sum of knowledge gained from such methods and discipline.
Knowledge derived from scientific disciplines, scientific method, or any systematic effort.
(collective) The scientific community.
To cause to become versed in science; to make skilled; to instruct.
To use science to solve a problem.
The sport of boxing.
To cause (something) to move rapidly in opposite directions alternatingly.
definitions.example: He shook the can of soda for thirty seconds before delivering it to me, so that, when I popped it open, soda went everywhere.

definitions.definition: To move (one's head) from side to side, especially to indicate refusal, reluctance or disapproval.
definitions.example: Shaking his head, he kept repeating "No, no, no".

definitions.definition: To move or remove by agitating; to throw off by a jolting or vibrating motion.
definitions.example: to shake fruit down from a tree

definitions.definition: To disturb emotionally; to shock.
definitions.example: He was shaken by what had happened.

definitions.definition: To lose, evade, or get rid of (something).
definitions.example: I can't shake the feeling that I forgot something.

definitions.definition: To move from side to side.
definitions.example: She shook with grief.

definitions.definition: (usually as "shake on") To shake hands.
definitions.example: OK, let's shake on it.

definitions.definition: To dance.
definitions.example: She was shaking it on the dance floor.

definitions.definition: To give a tremulous tone to; to trill.
definitions.example: to shake a note in music

definitions.definition: To threaten to overthrow.
definitions.example: The experience shook my religious belief.

definitions.definition: To be agitated; to lose firmness.
A movement that shakes.
A three dimensional work of art created by shaping malleable objects and letting them harden or by chipping away pieces from a rock (sculpting).
Works of art created by sculpting, as a group.
The three-dimensional ornamentation on the outer surface of a shell
To fashion something into a three-dimensional figure.
To represent something in sculpture.
To change the shape of a land feature by erosion etc.
To cause to be consumed by fire.
definitions.example: He burned his manuscript in the fireplace.

definitions.definition: To be consumed by fire, or in flames.
definitions.example: He watched the house burn.

definitions.definition: To overheat so as to make unusable.
definitions.example: He burned the toast. The blacksmith burned the steel.

definitions.definition: To become overheated to the point of being unusable.
definitions.example: The grill was too hot and the steak burned.

definitions.definition: To make or produce by the application of fire or burning heat.
definitions.example: to burn a hole;  to burn letters into a block

definitions.definition: To injure (a person or animal) with heat or chemicals that produce similar damage.
definitions.example: She burned the child with an iron, and was jailed for ten years.

definitions.definition: To cauterize.
To sunburn.
definitions.example: She forgot to put on sunscreen and burned.

definitions.definition: To consume, injure, or change the condition of, as if by action of fire or heat; to affect as fire or heat does.
definitions.example: to burn the mouth with pepper

definitions.definition: To be hot, e.g. due to embarrassment.
definitions.example: The child's forehead was burning with fever.  Her cheeks burned with shame.

definitions.definition: To cause to combine with oxygen or other active agent, with evolution of heat; to consume; to oxidize.
definitions.example: A human being burns a certain amount of carbon at each respiration.  to burn iron in oxygen

definitions.definition: To combine energetically, with evolution of heat.
definitions.example: Copper burns in chlorine.

definitions.definition: To write data to a permanent storage medium like a compact disc or a ROM chip.
definitions.example: We’ll burn this program onto an EEPROM one hour before the demo begins.

definitions.definition: To betray.
definitions.example: The informant burned him.

definitions.definition: To insult or defeat.
definitions.example: I just burned you again.

definitions.definition: To waste (time); to waste money or other resources.
definitions.example: The company has burned more than a million dollars a month this year.

definitions.definition: In certain games, to approach near to a concealed object which is sought.
definitions.example: You're cold... warm... hot... you're burning!

definitions.definition: To accidentally touch a moving stone.
In pontoon, to swap a pair of cards for another pair, or to deal a dead card.
To increase the exposure for certain areas of a print in order to make them lighter (compare dodge).
(of an element) To be converted to another element in a nuclear fusion reaction, especially in a star
To discard.
To shoot someone with a firearm.
Any animal other than a human; usually only applied to land vertebrates, especially large or dangerous four-footed ones.
(more specific) A domestic animal, especially a bovine farm animal.
A person who behaves in a violent, antisocial or uncivilized manner.
Anything regarded as larger or more powerful than one of its normal size or strength.
definitions.example: That is a beast of a stadium.

definitions.definition: Someone who is particularly impressive, especially athletically or physically.
(prisons) A sex offender.
Something unpleasant and difficult.
A thing or matter, especially a difficult or unruly one.
To impose arduous exercises, either as training or as punishment.
(chiefly Midwestern and northeastern US) great; excellent; powerful
A finely cut gemstone, especially a diamond, cut in a particular form with numerous facets so as to maximize light return through the top (called "table") of the stone.
The size of type between excelsior and diamond, standardized as 4-point.
Most hummingbird species of the genus Heliodoxa.
A kind of cotton goods, figured on the weaving.
Shining brightly.
definitions.example: the brilliant lights along the promenade

definitions.definition: (of a colour) Both bright and saturated.
definitions.example: butterflies with brilliant blue wings

definitions.definition: (of a voice or sound) Having a sharp, clear tone
Of surpassing excellence.
definitions.example: The actor's performance in the play was simply brilliant.

definitions.definition: Magnificent or wonderful.
Highly intelligent.
definitions.example: She is a brilliant scientist.

definitions.definition: A crazed Norse warrior who fought in a frenzy; a berserker.
Injuriously, maniacally, or furiously violent or out of control.
definitions.example: After seeing his sister stabbed to death, he went berserk and attacked the killer like a wild animal.

definitions.definition: Weird; bizarre.
To fasten with a band.
To fasten an identifying band around the leg of (a bird).
To group together for a common purpose; to confederate.
To group (students) together by perceived ability; to stream.
A pattern of band-like markings.
definitions.example: You can recognize this species of bird by the banding on the wing.

definitions.definition: A technique used in the study of wild birds, by attaching a small, individually numbered, metal or plastic tag to their legs or wings.
A division into bands or brackets.
definitions.example: property tax banding

definitions.definition: The formation of a band or brigade.
Someone or something that jumps, e.g. a participant in a jumping event in track or skiing.
A person who attempts suicide by jumping from a great height.
A short length of electrical conductor, to make a temporary connection. Also jump wire.
A removable connecting pin on an electronic circuit board.

A long drilling tool used by masons and quarry workers, consisting of an iron bar with a chisel-edged steel tip at one or both ends, operated by striking it against the rock, turning it slightly with each blow.
A crude kind of sleigh, usually a simple box on runners which are in one piece with the poles that form the thills.
A jumping spider
The larva of the cheese fly.
(18th century) One of certain Calvinistic Methodists in Wales whose worship was characterized by violent convulsions.
A spring to impel the star wheel, or a pawl to lock fast a wheel, in a repeating timepiece.
A shot in which the player releases the ball at the highest point of a jump; a jump shot.
A nuclear power plant worker who repairs equipment in areas with extremely high levels of radiation.
To connect with an electrical jumper.
The act of judging.
The power or faculty of performing such operations; especially, when unqualified, the faculty of judging or deciding rightly, justly, or wisely
definitions.example: a man of judgment

definitions.definition: The conclusion or result of judging; an opinion; a decision.
The act of determining, as in courts of law, what is conformable to law and justice; also, the determination, decision, or sentence of a court, or of a judge.
The final award; the last sentence.
A bottle of champagne or Burgundy wine containing 3 liters of fluid, four times the volume of a standard bottle.
A bottle of Bordeaux wine containing 4.5 liters of fluid, six times the volume of a standard bottle.
A medieval Scandinavian nobleman, especially in Norway and Denmark.
In a joking manner.
Something having a minor flaw
(grammar) a tense of verbs used in describing a past action that is incomplete or continuous
To make imperfect
Not perfect
Unisexual: having either male (with stamens) or female (with pistil) flowers, but not with both.
Known or expected to be polyphyletic, as of a form taxon.
Lacking some elementary organ that is essential to successful or normal activity.
(grammar) belonging to a tense of verbs used in describing a past action that is incomplete or continuous
To fish through a hole cut into ice.
Any member of notothenioid family Channichthyidae; these Antarctic fishes are named for the lack of hemoglobin in their blood
The noodlefish (family Salangidae in order Osmeriformes).
To push or pump (something, especially fluids) into a cavity or passage.
definitions.example: The nurse injected a painkilling drug into the veins of my forearm.

definitions.definition: To introduce (something) suddenly or violently.
definitions.example: Punk injected a much-needed sense of urgency into the British music scene.

definitions.definition: To administer an injection to (someone or something), especially of medicine or drugs.
definitions.example: Now lie back while we inject you with the anesthetic.

definitions.definition: To take or be administered something by means of injection, especially medicine or drugs.
definitions.example: It's been a week since I stopped injecting, and I'm still in withdrawal.

definitions.definition: To introduce (code) into an existing program or its memory space, often without tight integration and sometimes through a security vulnerability.
To cast or throw; used with on.
A glare in the sky caused by reflection of light from an ice field.
A beginning, an immature start.
To begin or start (something).
To cause or bring about.
To make a start.
Recently started but not fully formed yet; just begun; only elementary or immature.
Chaotic, disordered, confused; also, incoherent, rambling.
Of a crime, imposing criminal liability for an incompleted act.
The perception of noise, such as a ringing or beating sound, which has no external source.
A sparkle or glimmer of light
A sparkle of delight in the eyes.
definitions.example: He was a rotund, jolly man with a twinkle in his eye.

definitions.definition: A flitting movement
A brief moment; a twinkling.
The female genitalia.
definitions.example: The popular Swedish cartoon song about genitals was translated as "Willie and Twinkle".

definitions.definition: (of a source of light) to shine with a flickering light; to glimmer
definitions.example: We could see the lights of the village twinkling in the distance.

definitions.definition: (chiefly of eyes) to be bright with delight
definitions.example: His shrewd little eyes twinkled roguishly.

definitions.definition: To bat, blink or wink the eyes
To flit to and fro
A person who makes, repairs, or alters clothes professionally, especially suits and men's clothing.
definitions.example: He works as a tailor on Swanston Street.

definitions.definition: The bluefish (Pomatomus saltatrix).
To make, repair, or alter clothes.
definitions.example: We can tailor that jacket for you if you like.

definitions.definition: To make or adapt (something) for a specific need.
definitions.example: The website was tailored to the client's needs.

definitions.definition: To restrict (something) in order to meet a particular need.
definitions.example: a narrowly tailored law

definitions.definition: A telecommunication device (originally mechanical, and now electronic) used for two-way talking with another person (now often shortened to phone).
The game of Chinese whispers.
To (attempt to) contact someone using the telephone.
To convey (a message) by telephoning.
Possessing tact; able to deal with people in a sensitive manner.
To make a loud, deep cry, especially from pain, anger, or other strong emotion.
To laugh in a particularly loud manner.
definitions.example: The audience roared at his jokes.

definitions.definition: Of animals (especially the lion), to make a loud deep noise.
definitions.example: The lioness roared to scare off the hyenas.

definitions.definition: Generally, of inanimate objects etc., to make a loud resounding noise.
To proceed vigorously.
To cry aloud; to proclaim loudly.
To be boisterous; to be disorderly.
To make a loud noise in breathing, as horses do when they have a certain disease.
(North Midlands) to cry
A loud, deep, prolonged sound, as of a large beast; a roar.
An affection of the windpipe of a horse, causing a loud, peculiar noise in breathing under exertion.
(North Midlands) to cry
Intensive; extreme.
Very successful; lively.
definitions.example: The ice-cream sellers did a roaring trade in the midday heat.

definitions.definition: A steady look, a gaze.
One's concern for another; esteem; relation, reference.
(preceded by “in” or “with”) A particular aspect or detail; respect, sense.
The worth or estimation in which something or someone is held.
definitions.example: He is held in great regard in Whitehall.

definitions.definition: Emotional pain on account of something done or experienced in the past, with a wish that it had been different; a looking back with dissatisfaction or with longing.
Dislike; aversion.
To feel sorry about (a thing that has or has not happened), afterthink: to wish that a thing had not happened, that something else had happened instead.
definitions.example: He regretted his words.

definitions.definition: (more generally) To feel sorry about (any thing).
definitions.example: I regret that I have to do this, but I don't have a choice.

definitions.definition: To miss; to feel the loss or absence of.
A small brook or stream; a streamlet.
definitions.example: A rivulet of tears ran down his face.

definitions.definition: Perizoma affinitatum, a geometrid moth.
Rite; a repeated set of actions
definitions.example: They carried out the ritual carefully.

definitions.definition: Related to a rite or repeated set of actions.
definitions.example: They performed the ritual lighting of the candles.

definitions.definition: Person living or originating from Europe
Person who resides within the European Union
The currency unit of the European Monetary Union. Symbol: €
A coin with a face value of 1 euro.
That which emits something.
One terminal of a bipolar transistor (BJT).
One chosen or set apart.
In Calvinist theology, one foreordained to Heaven. In other Christian theologies, someone chosen by God for salvation.
To choose or make a decision (to do something)
To choose (a candidate) in an election
Who has been elected in a specified post, but has not yet entered office.
definitions.example: He is the President elect.

definitions.definition: Chosen; taken by preference from among two or more.
A shift (scheduled work period) that takes place early in the day.
At a time in advance of the usual or expected event.
definitions.example: at eleven, we went for an early lunch;  she began reading at an early age;  his mother suffered an early death

definitions.definition: Arriving a time before expected; sooner than on time.
definitions.example: The early guests sipped their punch and avoided each other's eyes.

definitions.definition: Near the start or beginning.
definitions.example: Early results showed their winning 245 out of 300 seats in parliament. The main opponent locked up only 31 seats.

definitions.definition: Having begun to occur; in its early stages.
definitions.example: early cancer

definitions.definition: Of a star or class of stars, hotter than the sun.
At a time before expected; sooner than usual.
definitions.example: We finished the project an hour sooner than scheduled, so we left early.

definitions.definition: Soon; in good time; seasonably.
An image or representation of an idea; a representation of an ideal form; an apparition of some actual or imaginary entity, or of some aspect of reality.
A phantom, a ghost or elusive entity.
The bell that summons worshipers to vespers; the vesper-bell
The evening.
A vesper martini.

A vent or fissure on the surface of a planet (usually in a mountainous form) with a magma chamber attached to the mantle of a planet or moon, periodically erupting forth lava and volcanic gases onto the surface.
A form of firework.
A type of parchment paper made from the skin of a lamb, baby goat, or calf.
A writing paper of very high quality.
A grape plantation, especially one used in the production of wine.
definitions.example: The vineyard of Château Margaux stands as the producer of one of the world's greatest and most sought-after red wines.

definitions.definition: A loose robe or outer garment worn historically by men in Arab or Middle Eastern countries.
A sleeveless garment that buttons down the front, worn over a shirt, and often as part of a suit; a waistcoat.
A sleeveless garment, often with a low-cut neck, usually worn under a shirt or blouse.
A sleeveless top, typically with identifying colours or logos, worn by an athlete or member of a sports team.
Any sleeveless outer garment, often for a purpose such as identification, safety, or storage.
A vestment.
Clothing generally; array; garb.
To clothe with, or as with, a vestment, or garment; to dress; to robe; to cover, surround, or encompass closely.
To clothe with authority, power, etc.; to put in possession; to invest; to furnish; to endow; followed by with and the thing conferred.
definitions.example: to vest a court with power to try cases of life and death

definitions.definition: To place or give into the possession or discretion of some person or authority; to commit to another; with in before the possessor.
definitions.example: The power of life and death is vested in the king, or in the courts.

definitions.definition: To invest; to put.
definitions.example: to vest money in goods, land, or houses

definitions.definition: To clothe with possession; also, to give a person an immediate fixed right of present or future enjoyment of.
definitions.example: an estate is vested in possession

definitions.definition: (of an inheritance or a trust fund) To devolve upon the person currently entitled when a prior interest has ended.
definitions.example: Upon the death of the Sovereign the Crown automatically vests in the next heir without the need of coronation or other formality.

definitions.definition: (financial) To become vested, to become permanent.
definitions.example: My pension vests at the end of the month and then I can take it with me when I quit.

definitions.definition: Of, or relating to heraldry or heralds.
To take a long walk for pleasure or exercise.
definitions.example: Don't forget to bring the map when we go hiking tomorrow.

definitions.definition: To unfairly or suddenly raise a price.
To snap the ball to start a play.
To lean out to the windward side of a sailboat in order to counterbalance the effects of the wind on the sails.
To pull up or tug upwards sharply.
definitions.example: She hiked her skirt up.

definitions.definition: Walking in the countryside for pleasure or sport.
A kingfisher said in classical mythology to nest on the sea, thereby calming the waters.
A tropical kingfisher of the genus Halcyon, such as the sacred kingfisher (Halcyon sancta) of Australia.
Pertaining to the halcyon or kingfisher.
Calm, undisturbed, peaceful, serene.
Thick or obscured with haze.
definitions.example: a hazy view of the polluted city street

definitions.definition: Not clear or transparent.
Obscure; confused; not clear.
definitions.example: a hazy argument

definitions.definition: To possess, own.
definitions.example: I have a house and a car.

definitions.definition: To hold, as something at someone's disposal.
definitions.example: Do you have the key? (not necessarily one's own key)

definitions.definition: Used to state the existence or presence of someone in a specified relationship with the subject.
definitions.example: I have a really mean boss.

definitions.definition: To partake of (a particular substance, especially food or drink, or action or activity).
definitions.example: Can I have a look at that?

definitions.definition: To be scheduled to attend, undertake or participate in.
definitions.example: Fred won't be able to come to the party; he has a meeting that day.

definitions.definition: To experience, go through, undergo.
definitions.example: He had surgery on his hip yesterday.

definitions.definition: To be afflicted with, suffer from.
definitions.example: He had a cold last week.

definitions.definition: (auxiliary verb, taking a past participle) Used in forming the perfect aspect.
definitions.example: I had already eaten.

definitions.definition: Used as an interrogative verb before a pronoun to form a tag question, echoing a previous use of 'have' as an auxiliary verb or, in certain cases, main verb. (For further discussion, see the appendix English tag questions.)
definitions.example: They haven't eaten dinner yet, have they?

definitions.definition: (auxiliary verb, taking a to-infinitive) See have to.
definitions.example: I have to go.

definitions.definition: To give birth to.
definitions.example: My mother had me when she was 25.

definitions.definition: To engage in sexual intercourse with.
definitions.example: He's always bragging about how many women he's had.

definitions.definition: To accept as a romantic partner.
definitions.example: Despite my protestations of love, she would not have me.

definitions.definition: (transitive with bare infinitive) To cause to, by a command, request or invitation.
definitions.example: They had me feed their dog while they were out of town.

definitions.definition: (transitive with adjective or adjective-phrase complement) To cause to be.
definitions.example: He had him arrested for trespassing.

definitions.definition: (transitive with bare infinitive) To be affected by an occurrence. (Used in supplying a topic that is not a verb argument.)
definitions.example: I've had three people today tell me my hair looks nice.

definitions.definition: (transitive with adjective or adjective-phrase complement) To depict as being.
definitions.example: Their stories differed; he said he'd been at work when the incident occurred, but her statement had him at home that entire evening.

definitions.definition: To defeat in a fight; take.
definitions.example: I could have him!

definitions.definition: (obsolete outside Ireland) To be able to speak (a language).
definitions.example: I have no German.

definitions.definition: To feel or be (especially painfully) aware of.
definitions.example: Dan certainly has arms today, probably from scraping paint off four columns the day before.

definitions.definition: To trick, to deceive.
definitions.example: You had me alright! I never would have thought that was just a joke.

definitions.definition: (often with present participle) To allow; to tolerate.
definitions.example: I asked my dad if I could go to the concert this Thursday, but he wouldn't have it since it's a school night.

definitions.definition: (often used in the negative) To believe, buy, be taken in by.
definitions.example: I made up an excuse as to why I was out so late, but my wife wasn't having any of it.

definitions.definition: To host someone; to take in as a guest.
definitions.example: Thank you for having me!

definitions.definition: To get a reading, measurement, or result from an instrument or calculation.
definitions.example: I have two contacts on my scope.

definitions.definition: (of a jury) To consider a court proceeding that has been completed; to begin deliberations on a case.
definitions.example: We'll schedule closing arguments for Thursday, and the jury will have the case by that afternoon.

definitions.definition: To make an observation of (a bird species).
A person who fails to show gratitude; an ingrate.
Not grateful; not expressing gratitude.
To remove a connection to ground potential.
definitions.example: If you unground the extension cord it will cause a shock hazard in that drill.

definitions.definition: To free from the punishment of being grounded (restricted to home).
definitions.example: We'll consider ungrounding you when you can act your age.

definitions.definition: Not grounded; without ground or basis, unreal, false.
definitions.example: The defendant's claims are fictitious; they are entirely ungrounded in fact.

definitions.definition: Not connected to ground potential
The act of rising; appearance above the horizon; rising.
To rise; to get up; to appear from below the horizon.
To have an upward direction or inclination
To rebel or revolt; to take part in an uprising.
Ultraviolet colour.
Of electromagnetic radiation beyond (higher in frequency than) light visible to the human eye; radiation with wavelengths from 380 nanometre - 10 nanometre
Any of several arboreal monkeys, of the genus Cacajao, from the Amazon basin.
Meal made from rolled or round oats.
A breakfast cereal made from rolled oats, cooked in milk and/or water.
A light greyish brown colour, like that of oatmeal.
Of a light greyish brown colour, like that of oatmeal
A woodwind musical instrument that is closed at both sides to produce an enclosed space, and punctured with finger holes.
A monocotyledonous plant (Allium cepa), allied to garlic, used as vegetable and spice.
The bulb of such a plant.
The genus as a whole.
A ball.
A person from Bermuda or of Bermudian descent.
The branch of zoology that deals with the scientific study of birds.
Something that is oval in shape.
Shaped like an oval.
Egg-shaped; shaped like an oval, but more tapered at one end; ovate.
To remove, get rid of or erase, especially written or printed material, or data on a computer or other device.
A measure of the mass of matter contained by a unit volume.
The ratio of one quantity, representing something of interest, to another quantity representing space, area, or extent in which the thing of interest is distributed.
definitions.example: The number of particles per unit volume of a specified volume can be considered to be the particle density for the specified volume.

definitions.definition: The probability that an outcome will fall into a given range, per unit of that range; the relative likelihood of possible values of a continuous random variable.
Stupidity; denseness.
A performer's first performance to the public, in sport, the arts or some other area.
The first public presentation of a theatrical play, motion picture, opera, musical composition, dance, or other performing arts piece.
definitions.example: Since making its debut two years ago, the program has gained cult status.

definitions.definition: The first appearance of a debutante in society.
To formally introduce, as to the public
definitions.example: Amalgamated Software Systems debuted release 3.2 in Spring of 2004.

definitions.definition: To make one's initial formal appearance
definitions.example: Release 3.2 debuted to mixed reviews in Spring of 2004.

definitions.definition: An insect of the suborder Epiprocta or, more strictly, the infraorder Anisoptera, having four long transparent wings held perpendicular to a long body when perched.
A device that is used for lifting and moving large objects.
A framework that is constructed over a mine or oil well for the purpose of boring or lowering pipes.
A hangman.
To hoist with, or as if with, a derrick.
To remove (a pitcher).
